hero.title: Transform Images to
hero.subtitle: A complete pipeline from DUSt3R depth estimation to real-time 3D rendering. No camera calibration required.

features.title: Powerful Features
features.subtitle: Everything you need to transform images into stunning 3D scenes

depth.title: Depth Estimation
depth.description: Automatically estimate depth maps from images with state-of-the-art DUSt3R model. No calibration or depth sensors required.

pose.title: Camera Pose
pose.description: Multi-view consistent camera pose estimation through global alignment optimization. Recovers both intrinsic and extrinsic parameters.

colmap.title: COLMAP Export
colmap.description: Standard COLMAP format export (cameras.bin, images.bin, points3D.bin), compatible with all major 3DGS/NeRF frameworks.

render.title: Real-time Rendering
render.description: Real-time rendering powered by gsplat with built-in pruning optimization. Achieve 60+ FPS on consumer GPUs.

quality.title: Quality Evaluation
quality.description: Built-in quality assessment metrics including depth consistency, point cloud density, and reconstruction completeness.

cross.title: Cross Platform
cross.description: Works on Windows, Linux, and macOS. Supports both NVIDIA and AMD GPUs through CUDA and ROCm backends.

workflow.title: How It Works
workflow.subtitle: From images to interactive 3D scenes in just a few steps

step1.title: 1. Image Input
step1.desc: Load 24+ images from different viewpoints. Support JPG, PNG, and other common formats. Images are automatically resized to optimal resolution.

step2.title: 2. DUSt3R Processing
step2.desc: Run DUSt3R inference on image pairs to estimate relative depth maps and camera poses. Uses ViT-Large backbone for best quality.

step3.title: 3. Global Alignment
step3.desc: Optimize all views jointly for consistent 3D reconstruction. Handles scale ambiguity and produces metric point cloud.

step4.title: 4. COLMAP Export
step4.desc: Export cameras, images, and 3D points in COLMAP format. Ready for downstream 3DGS training or other applications.

step5.title: 5. 3DGS Training
step5.desc: Train 3D Gaussian Splatting model with built-in pruning. Achieve high quality with fewer Gaussians through adaptive density control.

step6.title: 6. Interactive Viewer
step6.desc: Real-time rendering with gsplat viewer. Navigate freely through the reconstructed scene at 60+ FPS.

demo.title: See It in Action
demo.subtitle: From input images to stunning 3D reconstruction

benchmark.title: Performance Benchmark
benchmark.subtitle: Tested on RTX 5060 Laptop GPU with 24 images at 512px resolution

q1.q: What types of scenes work best?
q1.a: The pipeline works best with textured scenes captured from multiple viewpoints. Avoid texture-less surfaces (white walls, glass) and highly reflective objects. Indoor scenes, outdoor landscapes, and objects with good texture detail produce the best results.

q2.q: How many images do I need?
q2.a: Minimum 24 images are recommended for good quality. More images generally improve reconstruction quality, especially for complex scenes. Images should have good overlap between adjacent views (at least 30-50% overlap).

q3.a: NVIDIA GPU with CUDA support is recommended. RTX 30/40/50 series work great. Minimum 4GB VRAM for 512px resolution, 8GB+ recommended for higher resolutions. AMD GPUs are supported through ROCm on Linux.

q4.q: Why RTX 50 series requires building gsplat from source?
q4.a: Pre-built gsplat wheels don't support sm_120 (Blackwell) architecture yet. Simply build from source with: pip install git+https://github.com/nerfstudio-project/gsplat.git --no-build-isolation

q5.q: Can I use this for commercial projects?
q5.a: DUSt3R is licensed under CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 for non-commercial use. For commercial applications, please check the original DUSt3R license and consider contacting NAVER for commercial licensing options.

q6.q: How does quality compare to COLMAP?
q6.a: For scenes with good image coverage, the quality is comparable to COLMAP. The main advantage is speed - our pipeline completes in minutes vs hours for COLMAP SfM. For challenging scenes (low texture, sparse views), COLMAP may still produce better results.

quickstart.title: Quick Start
quickstart.subtitle: Get up and running in minutes

install.title: 2. Install Dependencies
install.code:
# Install PyTorch with CUDA
pip install torch torchvision --index-url https://download.pytorch.org/whl/cu128

# Install other dependencies
pip install -r requirements.txt

# Build DUSt3R CUDA extension
cd dust3r/croco/models/curope
python setup.py build_ext --inplace
cd ../../../..

# Install gsplat (build from source)
pip install git+https://github.com/nerfstudio-project/gsplat.git --no-build-isolation
